description: v6.0 Single Position Architecture - Complete overhaul of signal generation to enforce one-trade-at-a-time, position tracking, range mode HTF validation, 60s cooldown, and Python-side P&L estimation for AutoOptimizer learning.
steps: Step 1: In python_bridge/config/settings.py SignalConfig dataclass: Change `cooldown_seconds: int = 10` to `cooldown_seconds: int = 60`. Add `max_hold_seconds: int = 300` field. Add `max_positions: int = 1` field.

Step 2: In python_bridge/strategies/signal_generator.py __init__(): Add `self._active_position = None` attribute. This will store a dict with keys: direction (BUY/SELL), entry_price (float), entry_time (float from time.time()), signal_context (dict with confidence, session, sl_distance, momentum_lookback, rsi_at_entry).

Step 3: In python_bridge/strategies/signal_generator.py generate_signal(): At the VERY START (after hold_signal definition, BEFORE cooldown check), add position management logic: (a) If self._active_position is not None: check if cooldown (60s from entry_time) hasn't passed -> return HOLD. Check if max_hold_seconds (300s) exceeded OR momentum reversed (current momentum_direction != active_position direction) -> close position (compute estimated PnL = (current_price - entry_price) for BUY or (entry_price - current_price) for SELL, feed to auto_optimizer via new record_estimated_trade method, log the closure, set self._active_position = None, and continue to generate a new signal). Otherwise (position open, within hold time, momentum same direction) -> return HOLD with log message 'letting position ride'. (b) Only proceed to generate a new signal if self._active_position is None.

Step 4: In python_bridge/strategies/signal_generator.py generate_signal(): In the range mode section (where momentum_direction is set to BUY/SELL from range extremes), add HTF validation BLOCK: If range_signal == 'range_buy' AND htf_bias is not None AND htf_bias.get('5m', 0) < -0.5 AND htf_bias.get('15m', 0) < -0.5 -> log 'RANGE BUY BLOCKED: M5 and M15 both bearish' and set range_signal = None (which will trigger the existing 'if range_signal is None: return hold_signal' logic). Same for range_sell: if htf_bias.get('5m', 0) > 0.5 AND htf_bias.get('15m', 0) > 0.5 -> block.

Step 5: In python_bridge/strategies/signal_generator.py generate_signal(): After the signal is generated (after self._last_signal_time = time.time()), set self._active_position = {'direction': action, 'entry_price': current_price, 'entry_time': time.time(), 'signal_context': {'confidence': timing_confidence, 'session': session, 'sl_distance': levels['sl_pips'] * 0.1, 'momentum_lookback': self.data_config.momentum_lookback, 'rsi_at_entry': current_rsi_for_zone if current_rsi_for_zone else 50.0}}.

Step 6: In python_bridge/strategies/auto_optimizer.py: Add a new method `record_estimated_trade(self, trade_context: Dict) -> None` that accepts the Python-estimated trade data (same format as record_trade: session, confidence, momentum_lookback, sl_distance, result_pnl, direction, rsi_at_entry, trail_tier='estimated', cooldown_used, max_positions_at_entry, entry_time, exit_time) and calls self.record_trade(trade_context) directly. This is a thin wrapper that tags the trade as 'estimated' in trail_tier.

Step 7: In python_bridge/main.py: Update VERSION = '6.0'. In the MT5 confirmation handling section (step 12), when a fill confirmation comes in (type == 'open' or type == 'fill'), ALSO update self.signal_generator._active_position entry_price with the actual fill price from MT5: `if self.signal_generator._active_position is not None: self.signal_generator._active_position['entry_price'] = entry_price`. When a close confirmation comes in (type == 'close'), clear the active position: `self.signal_generator._active_position = None`.

Step 8: In Python_Bridge_EA.mq5: Change `#property version "5.36"` to `#property version "6.00"`. Change the header comment from `//|  v5.3.6 - 24/7 Trading + No Limits + Fast Self-Learning` to `//|  v6.0 - Quality Over Quantity - Single Position Architecture     |`. Keep InpMaxOpenTrades = 5 unchanged (safety net).
Step 9: In python_bridge/strategies/auto_optimizer.py _default_params(): Change `'cooldown_seconds': 10` to `'cooldown_seconds': 60` and `'max_positions': 3` to `'max_positions': 1`.

Step 10: Update tests. In python_bridge/tests/test_auto_optimizer.py: Fix test_default_params to assert cooldown_seconds == 60 and max_positions == 1 (was previously expecting 120 and 1 respectively - needs to match new defaults). Fix test_cooldown_reduces_when_fast_profitable to reflect the new default of 60. Add a new test for record_estimated_trade method. In python_bridge/tests/test_signal_generator.py: Add tests for (a) active position blocks new signals, (b) position closed after max hold time, (c) range BUY blocked when HTF bearish, (d) active_position set after signal generation.
acceptance_criteria: Running `python3 -m pytest python_bridge/tests/ --tb=short` passes all tests (including updated and new tests)
signal_generator.py has self._active_position attribute that tracks the single open position
generate_signal() returns HOLD when _active_position is not None and cooldown/hold conditions not met
generate_signal() closes position (estimates PnL, feeds optimizer) when momentum reverses or max_hold exceeded
Range mode BUY is blocked when both M5 < -0.5 AND M15 < -0.5; range SELL blocked when both M5 > 0.5 AND M15 > 0.5
After generating a non-HOLD signal, _active_position is populated with direction, entry_price, entry_time, signal_context
Python_Bridge_EA.mq5 has version '6.00' and updated header comment
verification: Run `python3 -m pytest python_bridge/tests/ --tb=short` and confirm all tests pass
Run `python3 -c "from python_bridge.strategies.signal_generator import SignalGenerator; sg = SignalGenerator(); assert sg._active_position is None; print('OK')"` to confirm new attribute exists
Run `python3 -c "from python_bridge.strategies.auto_optimizer import AutoOptimizer; ao = AutoOptimizer(); assert hasattr(ao, 'record_estimated_trade'); print('OK')"` to confirm new method exists

Run `python3 -c "from python_bridge.config.settings import SignalConfig; sc = SignalConfig(); assert sc.cooldown_seconds == 60; assert sc.max_hold_seconds == 300; assert sc.max_positions == 1; print('OK')"` to confirm config changes
findings: Implementation complete. All 528 tests pass (1 pre-existing failure in test_smart_upgrades.py::TestConfigs::test_price_structure_config_defaults which asserts 0.15 but code has 0.05 - unrelated to this change). The previous pre-existing failures in test_auto_optimizer (cooldown==120, cooldown==119) are now fixed as part of this task. Key design notes: (1) _active_position is separate from _open_positions dict which is for RL agent tracking. (2) The position management block runs BEFORE the cooldown check in generate_signal(). (3) The HTF validation is inserted between range signal detection and the existing 'if range_signal is None: return hold_signal' check. (4) Auto-optimizer record_estimated_trade is a thin wrapper that ensures trail_tier='estimated' before calling record_trade.
